citation.description: _gc_config_unit sets --unset gc.auto + gc.autoDetach=false + maintenance.autoDetach=false (operator-approved alternative to maintenance.auto=false; same functional goal of no detached background maintenance). Proven by test_init_unsets_gc_auto_and_forces_foreground_maintenance in test_gc_config.py.

criteria.criterion: `_gc_config_unit` disables background auto-maintenance (`maintenance.auto=false` + `gc.auto=0`) so no detached `git maintenance`/`git gc` can run concurrently with writers; proven by a test asserting the store's effective config after `init`.

citation.description: ADR 0051 documents the decision: auto-gc stays enabled (bounded growth preserved) but forced foreground so it runs serialized under the write lock. sync.py docstring also explains this at lines 22-26.

criteria.criterion: Bounded-growth (WU-1) preserved via a serialized-under-write-lock gc/maintenance path, OR an explicit, documented decision to defer it.

citation.description: The test exists with only `pytestmark = pytest.mark.integration` (no skip/xfail decorator). The codebase-verifiable part (skip removed) is confirmed. CI green is not directly verifiable from the repo.

criteria.criterion: `test_enrich_queue_prune_never_drops_concurrent_locked_writes` re-enabled (skip removed) and green on the git-2.54 CI matrix (proving command: that test on CI).

citation.description: test_gc_config.py has test_init_unsets_gc_auto_and_forces_foreground_maintenance asserting new posture. test_ensure_drift_matrix.py lines 74-77 and 98-100 fabricate/assert gc-config drift. concurrency.md lines 382-395 contain CAVEAT about concurrent background gc. sync.py lines 19-26 contain the corrected explanation. init.py _gc_config_unit docstring (lines 96-124) corrects the 'gc safe by construction' claim.

criteria.criterion: gc-config + ensure-drift-matrix tests updated to the new posture; docs (sync.py, concurrency.md, init.py `_gc_config_unit`) corrected to state that concurrent background maintenance is unsafe.
